lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer local whenever privacy, sovereignty, or operator-briefing needs control
selection_reasons: Primary supervisor lane for local-only or sensitive work.
Should stay available even when bulk local capacity is being harvested.
avoid_when: A cheaper sovereign bulk lane can satisfy the task without consuming the supervisor reserve.

lanes.roi_posture: use local paid-for compute before cloud when quality is sufficient
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer local for private code, migrations, and maintenance unless cloud review materially changes the outcome
selection_reasons: Retains enough reserve for interactive coding while still allowing harvested local work.
avoid_when: The task is explicitly cloud-safe and benefits materially from premium frontier implementation quality.

lanes.roi_posture: maximize productive use of idle local capacity after reserves are protected
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: strongly prefer local for extraction, indexing, creative batches, and batch transforms
selection_reasons: Primary lane for harvested local compute and sovereign creative batch work.
Should absorb batch work that would otherwise spend cloud budget with no quality advantage.
avoid_when: Interactive reserves are degraded or a protected creative reserve is needed immediately.

lanes.roi_posture: save for high-leverage implementation and final-review work
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: use when the coding delta is worth premium frontier quality or faster issue resolution
selection_reasons: Primary premium coding lane for cloud-safe implementation.
Protected so the reserve is still available for high-value coding spikes.
avoid_when: The work is local-only or a sovereign local coding lane can finish it without meaningful quality loss.

lanes.roi_posture: burn early for large-context audits before spending scarcer premium coding reserve
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer for large-context, cloud-safe audits that would overconsume premium implementation lanes
selection_reasons: Primary large-context audit and architecture-review lane.
Burn before premium implementation reserve when the task is analysis-heavy.
avoid_when: The task contains sovereign-only, refusal-sensitive, or adult-sensitive material.

lanes.roi_posture: route deterministic refactors through the cheapest policy-legal model family that still meets quality
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer local or cheap metered routing for mechanical refactors before premium frontier spend
selection_reasons: Permanent repo mechanic for structured multi-file patching.
Separates mechanical refactor economics from premium interactive reasoning lanes.
avoid_when: The task is an open-ended architecture problem rather than a deterministic code mechanic.

lanes.roi_posture: spend subscription-backed GLM coding quota before metered overflow when the supported tooling path is healthy
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer for cloud-safe bulk coding and refactor overflow once support tooling is proven live
selection_reasons: Target subscription-backed overflow lane for cloud-safe coding.
Should sit between premium cloud lanes and cheap metered APIs when tooling health is green.
avoid_when: Provider auth or supported-tool evidence is stale or failed.

lanes.roi_posture: use as the cheapest cloud-safe bulk lane inside acceptable-quality thresholds
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer for low-cost cloud-safe transforms, extraction, and background throughput when local reserves are protected
selection_reasons: Cheapest metered cloud lane for eligible bulk work.
Ideal overflow path for classification, extraction, and cheap background transforms.
avoid_when: The work requires premium implementation quality or is not cloud-safe.

lanes.roi_posture: use for bounded async research or background cloud-safe processing after higher-value lanes are protected
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer local harvested capacity first unless async cloud breadth materially improves throughput
selection_reasons: Useful for deep research and async cloud-safe work, but not a protected premium reserve.
avoid_when: A local harvested lane can perform the work without privacy or quality loss.

lanes.roi_posture: retire after review coverage migrates cleanly to Gemini and Codex
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: current-state only until migration gates pass
selection_reasons: Retained only as a migration lane until review coverage fully transfers.
avoid_when: Target-state routing is available and healthy.

lanes.roi_posture: product runtime lane, not a general-purpose reasoning reserve
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: only use for public-product async work that already belongs on Vercel
selection_reasons: Purpose-built product async lane for webhooks, cache refreshes, and transactional product work.
avoid_when: The task is internal, private, sovereign, or better served by Athanor or local compute.

Failure class quota_exhausted has bounded fallbacks and recovery gate.
Failure class provider_auth_failed has bounded fallbacks and recovery gate.
Failure class model_lane_outage has bounded fallbacks and recovery gate.
Failure class litellm_degraded has bounded fallbacks and recovery gate.
Failure class gpu_capacity_hit has bounded fallbacks and recovery gate.
Failure class cluster_degraded_mode has bounded fallbacks and recovery gate.
Subscription family claude_max has collector and reserve floor.
Subscription family chatgpt_pro has collector and reserve floor.
Subscription family gemini_advanced has collector and reserve floor.
Subscription family kimi_allegretto has collector and reserve floor.
Planned subscription family glm_coding_plan has an activation gate.
Metered family deepseek_api has a daily budget ceiling.
Metered family zai_api has a daily budget ceiling.
Protected reserve foundry-rtx4090 is explicitly declared.
Protected reserve workshop-rtx5090 is explicitly declared.
Safe-surface summary inputs are present and scope denial for Athanor-adjacent paths is documented.
Override type single_run_force is declared in the active override policy.
Override type temporary_disable is declared in the active override policy.
Override type temporary_promote is declared in the active override policy.
Override type budget_raise is declared in the active override policy.
Override type collector_bypass is declared in the active override policy.

authority_classes.definition: Canonical Athanor implementation and adopted runtime-facing truth.
Devstack design, prototype, proving, and capability-forge truth.
Searchable Claude-era evidence that does not outrank current truth.

capability_stages.definition: Design intent exists but no proof artifact is ready.
Working slice exists but is not yet ready for Athanor adoption.
Capability has concrete evidence and is eligible for a promotion packet.
Capability is represented in Athanor canonical truth and may affect real behavior.
Capability is intentionally superseded and kept only for lineage.

capabilities.label: Agent Gateway Layer
capabilities.runtime_target: VAULT LiteLLM plus Athanor routing surfaces
acceptance_criteria: Athanor registries define canonical provider, routing, and model-lane truth.
Gateway updates land through Athanor control-plane surfaces instead of lingering only in devstack prose.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Revert the affected Athanor registry and runtime packet changes, then re-run the governed VAULT or vLLM runtime packets.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded devstack gateway design notes once Athanor surfaces remain canonical and current.
notes: Devstack remains the proving/configuration lane for future gateway work.
Athanor registries and docs remain the adopted-system truth.
The 2026-04-14 onboarding hardening delta is now accepted as canonical operator-surface truth and remains subordinate to the existing runtime packets.

capabilities.label: GraphRAG Hybrid Retrieval
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor governed knowledge subsystem: FOUNDRY GraphRAG retrieval service plus Athanor agent retrieval integration over the existing truth.docs and truth.graph namespaces
acceptance_criteria: Hybrid retrieval must run against real Athanor and devstack documents with stable proof artifacts.
The adoption target is fixed as a governed knowledge subsystem rather than a free-floating service proof.
Runtime deployment and rollback must land through explicit FOUNDRY GraphRAG and Athanor agents packet linkage before adoption.
approval_gated_remaining: Operator review and packet acceptance remain the only adoption gate; the governed runtime revision is already live and green, so no further compose-recovery or vector-path unblock work remains before widening beyond shadow evidence.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Disable the GraphRAG knowledge-subsystem delta by reverting Athanor retrieval integration and leaving the devstack proof service isolated behind the foundry-graphrag runtime lane.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Retain proof artifacts in devstack and archive superseded wrapper or integration-shape experiments now that the governed knowledge-subsystem landing zone is selected.
notes: The landing zone is now explicit: a governed knowledge subsystem rooted in the FOUNDRY GraphRAG service and Athanor agents integration surfaces.

The 2026-04-12 governed Foundry compose redeploy is now live, and the promotion eval reran green with valid shadow-tier evidence recorded in C:/Athanor/reports/truth-inventory/graphrag-promotion-eval.json.

The source-side Athanor agents retrieval seam is now landed in C:/Athanor/projects/agents plus the platform-topology service map, so the remaining boundary is governed runtime deployment rather than more design shaping.

The current retrieval revision is now live through the governed foundry-agents-compose-deploy-packet, and the promotion eval reran green with valid shadow-tier evidence recorded in C:/Athanor/reports/truth-inventory/graphrag-promotion-eval.json.
The 2026-04-13 wake-before-embed hardening closed the embedding sleep-state blocker, and the current hybrid proof now passes cleanly on route `hybrid` with valid shadow-tier evidence.

The next adopted delta is packet acceptance and ordinary retrieval quality follow-through; the runtime revision is already live and green, so no more compose-recovery or vector-path unblock work remains.

capabilities.label: Watchdog Runtime Guard
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor-owned FOUNDRY runtime guard live canary with packet-backed restart controls
acceptance_criteria: Pause, resume, restart, and remediation boundaries must be explicit and operator-safe.
Live runtime guard authority must remain packet-backed, bounded to Band A remediation, and protected-service safe.
Rollback and gate-closing paths must remain explicit before ordinary production widening.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Pause the watchdog or close WATCHDOG_MUTATIONS_ENABLED, or restore the previous /opt/athanor/watchdog bundle if the live Athanor-owned runtime guard widens mutation scope.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded MVW iterations after a single Athanor-bound packet path exists.
notes: The Athanor-owned watchdog bundle, runtime-ownership lane, and rollout packet now exist.
The 2026-04-14 live canary rollout is active on FOUNDRY with bounded non-protected remediation enabled.
The Athanor-side formal eval is green against both the focused route tests and the live canary probe.

capabilities.label: GPU Scheduler Extension
acceptance_criteria: Scheduler work must extend the existing Athanor GPU orchestrator instead of creating a competing control plane.
Runtime deployment must land through the existing governed packet and lane system.
approval_gated_remaining: Review and accept the promotion packet as the bounded scheduler-extension adoption path before widening beyond shadow evidence.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Keep the scheduler delta out of Athanor runtime until the packet is accepted; revert to the current orchestrator lane if a packeted rollout fails.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded scheduler design deltas once the chosen extension path is adopted.
notes: Extends an already-adopted Athanor surface, so promotion must land as a bounded delta rather than a second scheduler.
The runtime linkage is the existing foundry-gpu-orchestrator deployment lane plus a dedicated scheduler-state rollout packet for the new bounded delta.

The 2026-04-12 rerun now pins the live six-zone orchestrator current state, and live probes show /scheduler/state plus the bounded request/preload/release routes with write-capability posture enabled in runtime.

The bounded scheduler extension now has a valid formal promotion eval, and the canonical scheduler-state rollout packet is backfilled as executed from live evidence, so adoption is now packet-review plus bounded rollout governance rather than more scheduler implementation work.

capabilities.label: Creative Identity Pipeline
capabilities.runtime_target: Shadow-adopted sovereign creative boundary across EOQ and the reusable ComfyUI workflow pack
acceptance_criteria: Identity-consistent image generation, Drive artifact discipline, and I2V proof must be stable enough to packetize.
The adopted boundary must be clear across EOQ, ComfyUI workflows, and project packets.
EOQ consumes pinned workflow copies while reusable promoted templates live in projects/comfyui-workflows.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Keep the creative pipeline at the shadow-adopted boundary, revert packet acceptance, and move reusable templates back to devstack if the Athanor landing contract regresses.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded prompt, LoRA, and workflow experiments once a stable Athanor-bound packet exists.
notes: The Athanor landing boundary is now explicit: EOQ owns pinned copies and project-specific workflow variants, while projects/comfyui-workflows owns reusable promoted sovereign templates.

The 2026-04-15 rerun on session4_eoq_obsidian_balcony.png cleared the technical visibility gate, the formal creative promotion eval passes, and the packet is accepted as a shadow-adopted creative boundary.
Runtime rollout remains intentionally not linked; this tranche adopts the project boundary and routing contract without widening into live creative runtime authority.

capabilities.label: Goose Operator Shell
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor operator shell, dashboard routing recommendations, and shell-level execution policy
acceptance_criteria: Goose is scoped as the primary shell-level operator agent without becoming a second control plane.
Direct CLI, Goose-wrapped, and LiteLLM-routed execution boundaries are explicit.
MCP extension use is bounded by Athanor vendor-policy and sensitivity rules.
Operator-shell recommendations and failure fallbacks are visible in the atlas and dashboard surfaces.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Remove Goose from the preferred shell path, restore direct terminal plus specialist CLI routing as the default operator path, and keep Goose-specific logic outside Athanor adopted truth.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive temporary shell-comparison notes and superseded shell-selection scratch docs once the governed shell policy is adopted or rejected.
notes: Goose is the adopted bounded shell path, not a replacement control plane.
Promotion should stay bounded to orchestration, shell policy, and operator-surface guidance.
The boundary evidence now lives in C:/Athanor/reports/truth-inventory/goose-boundary-evidence.md.
The 2026-04-12 formal shell comparison is green at 8 of 8 once Goose is pinned to the intended LiteLLM DeepSeek lane instead of the weaker workstation-local default model.
The 2026-04-14 standing operator approval closes the remaining packet-review gate and promotes Goose into adopted shell truth.
The governed DESK helper is now the ordinary bounded shell handoff path; future changes stay packet-backed instead of drifting through workstation-local defaults.

capabilities.label: OpenHands Bounded Worker Lane
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor bounded worker-lane orchestration and governed coding-worker experiments
acceptance_criteria: OpenHands runs only as an explicitly selected worker lane.
Its MCP and tool surfaces remain bounded by Athanor policy and repo sensitivity.
Worker outputs are reproducible, reviewable, and do not accumulate competing control-plane state.
Failure fallback returns cleanly to Codex, Aider, or local sovereign lanes.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Demote OpenHands back to research-only status, remove it from preferred lane-selection output, and keep its artifacts as devstack-only pilot evidence.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive OpenHands-specific onboarding or experiment notes that are not needed for later pilot replay or rejection rationale.
notes: OpenHands is a bounded sidecar candidate, not a core stack replacement.
Any worker-lane adoption must remain subordinate to Athanor routing and sidecar retention policy.

capabilities.label: Protocol-First Builder Kernel
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor builder front door, routing kernel, worker selection, and verification orchestration
acceptance_criteria: Athanor exposes one builder front door instead of a visible bundle of equal tools.
The builder kernel remains thin and does not become a universal super-agent.
Workers return structured result packets instead of transcript fan-in.
Verification completes before success is reported.
Private and adult-sensitive local tasks fail closed against cloud transfer.
Goose remains bounded and OpenHands remains pilot-only unless later proof changes that status.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Demote the builder kernel back to devstack-only proof artifacts and preserve the existing Athanor shell and routing surfaces without adopting a new control layer.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive superseded builder-stack planning notes that still describe devstack as a permanent separate user-facing stack. Keep the proof brief, routing matrix, and scenario suite as lineage.
notes: This capability describes target Athanor behavior while remaining devstack-owned until adoption proof is complete.
The first proof step is a bounded route slice rather than a broad control-plane rewrite.

capabilities.label: Letta Memory Plane
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor memory-plane, context retention, and cross-session state governance
acceptance_criteria: Letta is used as an explicit memory plane, not a hidden parallel truth system.
Memory retention boundaries are compatible with Athanor privacy, sovereignty, and artifact-topology rules.
Cross-session state can be audited, replayed, and pruned under current governance rules.
Memory use improves operator continuity or agent quality on measured tasks.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Disable the pilot memory adapter and keep durable context on the current registry, packet, and repo-doc stack only.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive memory-plane comparison notes or prompt-state experiments that do not become part of the accepted memory contract.
notes: Letta is a memory-plane candidate only if it improves continuity without becoming another truth surface.

capabilities.label: Agent Governance Toolkit Policy Plane
capabilities.runtime_target: Athanor agent governance, trust, identity, and protocol-policy enforcement
acceptance_criteria: AGT can enforce or enrich existing Athanor policy classes without duplicating the whole control plane.
Trust, identity, and protocol governance produce measurable guardrail value for autonomous mutation or protocol bridging.
Governance events remain auditable through current operator-stream and decision-record surfaces.
Disabling the integration cleanly returns control to native Athanor policy enforcement.
source_safe_remaining: Do not draft a live Athanor adapter on the current single-scenario bundle.
Only reopen this lane if a second protocol-boundary scenario shows non-duplicative value over the native approval matrix without widening permissions.
capabilities.rollback_or_disable_path: Remove the AGT adapter or policy bridge and fall back to the existing Athanor approval, routing, and failure-governance contracts.
capabilities.archive_instructions: Archive protocol-governance comparison notes and unused bridge experiments that do not become part of the adopted governance layer.
notes: AGT is a policy-plane candidate, not a control-plane replacement.
The first implementation slice should stay narrow and protocol-boundary focused.
The current benchmark bundle shows a narrow bridge that adds only an explanatory policy verdict and stricter blocked actions.
The 2026-04-12 manual review concluded that this single approval-held-mutation scenario is still too redundant to justify a live adapter or release-tier advancement.

capability_adoption_registry.data_handling_profile_contract: Every capability must declare a governed handling profile aligned with its landing surface.

records.label: Letta Memory Plane
records.next_action: Wire LETTA_API_KEY, run the bounded continuity benchmark, and keep replayability and pruning explicit.
records.next_formal_gate: Wire the required formal-eval env vars: `LETTA_API_KEY`.

records.label: Agent Governance Toolkit Policy Plane
records.manual_review_summary: The narrow approval-held-mutation bundle preserves native Athanor approval and audit lineage, but it does not yet prove non-duplicative operational value strong enough to justify a bounded AGT adapter or release-tier advancement.
records.next_action: Leave this lane below adapter work on the current manual review, and only reopen it if a second protocol-boundary scenario proves unique value over native Athanor policy.
records.next_formal_gate: Keep this lane below adapter work unless a second protocol-boundary scenario shows non-duplicative value over native Athanor policy.

records.label: OpenHands Bounded Worker Lane
records.next_action: Expose the OpenHands command on DESK, clear the worker env wiring, and run the bounded-worker eval.
records.next_formal_gate: Install or expose `openhands` on the preferred pilot host.

operator_test_record.description: Checks the bounded Goose shell pilot contract, execution-mode policy, and local tool readiness without treating Goose as a second control plane.
notes: Goose capability contract is missing from the adoption registry.
Goose pilot targets host desk; local_available=true.
Goose remains bounded to shell orchestration only; packet review still gates adoption of the preferred daily shell path.
Blocking reasons: missing_capability_contract.
operator_test_record.title: Goose operator shell pilot

operator_test_record.description: Checks the bounded-worker pilot contract, sidecar retention policy, and local tool readiness for OpenHands.
notes: OpenHands is not available on host desk; inventory_status=missing.
Blocking reasons: missing_command:openhands.
operator_test_record.title: OpenHands bounded worker pilot

operator_test_record.description: Checks the continuity-memory pilot contract, artifact retention posture, and local tool readiness for Letta.
notes: This flow is operator-smoke evidence only; the repeat-session continuity benchmark still needs to run before Letta can claim formal pilot proof.
operator_test_record.title: Letta memory plane pilot

operator_test_record.description: Checks the AGT policy-bridge pilot contract against existing approval, audit, and decision-governance surfaces.
notes: This flow is operator-smoke evidence only; the AGT lane still depends on narrow contract-trace review rather than smoke proof alone.
operator_test_record.title: AGT policy plane pilot

command_probe.stderr_tail:
Missing LETTA_API_KEY
Run 'letta' in interactive mode to authenticate or export the missing environment variable

selection_profiles.selection_reason: Prefer premium implementation quality first, then fall back to sovereign local coding when privacy or reserve posture requires it.
Large-context review work belongs on Gemini first, with Codex as the premium fallback.
Prefer broad audit coverage and cheap overflow before consuming premium implementation reserves.
Route mechanical patching through Aider first and let the model family vary behind the router.
Cheap bulk work should spend the cheapest eligible lane first and only escalate when quality or availability requires it.
Private implementation stays local even when throughput drops.
Creative batch work stays sovereign and prefers harvestable local capacity.

planned_subscriptions.next_proof_step: Keep supported_tool_usage_observed fresh with periodic bounded refreshes; demote the lane if the proof regresses.
planned_subscriptions.proof_record_command: python C:/Athanor/scripts/record_supported_tool_usage.py --family-id glm_coding_plan --tool-name <tool> --request-surface <surface> --note "Bounded supported-tool request completed successfully."

providers.label: Athanor Local
notes: Primary sovereign execution lane.
Use for private, refusal-sensitive, and local-first automation.

providers.label: Claude Code
notes: Primary frontier architecture and final-review lane.

providers.label: Codex CLI
notes: Primary async cloud execution lane.

providers.label: Gemini CLI
notes: Preferred large-context audit lane.
Official plan and CLI limits drift independently; measure actual usage separately from plan pricing.

providers.label: Kimi Code
verification_steps: Verify the subscribed monthly tier or billing surface for `Kimi Code` from a current operator-visible source.
Keep this lane cost-unverified until the billing tier is proven from a current runtime-visible or operator-visible surface.
notes: Official docs confirm Kimi Code benefits are included in Kimi Membership, refresh on a 7-day rolling cycle, and are limited to personal development use.
The public Kimi docs used here still do not publish the exact subscribed membership price for this lane.
The exact subscribed monthly tier still needs runtime billing verification.
Live CLI probes reconfirmed `kimi --version` on DESK and DEV during the 2026-03-29 verification pass.

providers.label: Z.ai GLM Coding
verification_steps: Keep GLM Coding Plan in ordinary routing while refreshing supported-tool evidence on DESK or DEV.

Record future supported-tool refreshes with `python C:/Athanor/scripts/record_supported_tool_usage.py --family-id glm_coding_plan --tool-name <tool> --request-surface <surface> ...` so quota-truth can stay activation-proven.
Verify which public GLM Coding Plan tier is actually subscribed before treating any published USD price as this lane's monthly cost.
If supported-tool evidence regresses, demote the lane back out of ordinary auto-routing.
notes: Official Z.ai docs publish GLM Coding Plan floor prices starting at USD 10 per month for Lite and USD 30 per month for Pro.
The same docs describe 5-hour rolling and 7-day quota windows, and separate metered API pricing outside the coding plan.
Live routing is now enabled because supported-tool usage was observed through Codex on 2026-04-13.
Current activation posture is supported_tool_usage_observed, so the lane may participate in ordinary auto-routing while evidence stays fresh.

providers.label: Anthropic API
verification_steps: Keep the `claude` alias in the regular VAULT provider-specific probe refresh so Athanor can distinguish transient cooldown pressure from real secret or served-model drift.
If repeated 429-style cooldown failures persist, inspect the current LiteLLM fallback and routing posture before rotating Anthropic credentials blindly.
Only reopen secret-delivery or served-model remediation if `/v1/models` drops the `claude` alias again or the lane falls back into explicit auth failure.
notes: The 2026-04-16 live VAULT provider probe hit `request_failed` on the `claude` alias because the fallback path reported cooling-down pressure instead of a fresh secret-delivery regression.

providers.label: OpenAI API
verification_steps: Rotate or correct `OPENAI_API_KEY` in the live VAULT LiteLLM secret-delivery surface.
Recreate or redeploy the `litellm` container so the repaired env set is applied.
Re-probe the served model `gpt` and keep the lane demoted until the provider-specific completion succeeds.
notes: Latest live VAULT provider probe classified this lane as auth_failed even though OPENAI_API_KEY is now present in the container env.

providers.label: Codestral API
verification_steps: Keep the lane in ordinary routing only while provider-specific completion probes continue succeeding.
Re-probe `codestral` after any VAULT LiteLLM config or secret-source change.
If a future probe fails, demote the lane and route it back into the VAULT repair packet.
notes: Codestral is currently provider-specific observed through the VAULT LiteLLM proxy.
MISTRAL_API_KEY is now present in the container env, so the remaining observed lane does not depend on a partial missing-env contract.

providers.label: Gemini API
verification_steps: Rotate or correct the canonical Gemini credential path already present in the live VAULT LiteLLM secret-delivery surface without widening the env surface.
Keep `GEMINI_API_KEY` and `GOOGLE_API_KEY` scoped to the current VAULT auth path unless operator review decides one should become the single canonical env name.
Recreate or redeploy the `litellm` container only if the VAULT env surface changes again.
Re-probe the served model `gemini` and keep the lane demoted until the provider-specific completion succeeds.
notes: Latest live VAULT provider probe now shows Gemini upstream returning explicit invalid-key errors while GEMINI_API_KEY and GOOGLE_API_KEY are both present in the container env.
Treat the current failure as a present-key validity problem first; only reopen auth-mode mismatch if a rotated or corrected key still fails on the same served alias.

providers.label: DeepSeek API
verification_steps: Keep the lane in ordinary routing only while provider-specific completion probes continue succeeding.
Re-probe `deepseek` after any VAULT LiteLLM config or secret-source change.
If a future probe fails, demote the lane and route it back into the VAULT repair packet.
notes: DeepSeek is configured as an API lane via LiteLLM.
Provider-specific completion proof is current through 2026-04-16.

providers.label: Moonshot API
verification_steps: Treat the provider-specific proof as current for the served model `kimi-k2.5` while the direct Moonshot env contract remains unverified.
Do not promote this lane as a direct-provider contract until Athanor chooses whether the observed `kimi-k2.5` path remains canonical or restores a direct Moonshot env-backed contract.
If the direct Moonshot lane becomes authoritative later, restore `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` and re-probe against the chosen direct served model.
notes: Moonshot still exposes public API pricing through a pricing-specific official post rather than a dedicated stable pricing reference page.

Latest live VAULT provider proof records successful `kimi-k2.5` completions, so the missing direct `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` env contract is tracked as optional cleanup until Athanor chooses to make the direct Moonshot lane authoritative again.

providers.label: DashScope Qwen API
verification_steps: Repair missing `DASHSCOPE_API_KEY` delivery in the live VAULT LiteLLM container env surface before attempting blind key rotation.
Recreate or redeploy the `litellm` container so the repaired env set is applied.
Re-probe the served model `qwen-max` and keep the lane demoted until the provider-specific completion succeeds.
notes: Official Model Studio pricing is published on the per-model list and can vary by deployment region.

Latest live VAULT provider probe classified this lane as auth_failed, and the current env audit shows `DASHSCOPE_API_KEY` absent from both container delivery and config-referenced resolution on the live surface.

providers.label: Venice API
verification_steps: Restore usable Venice API balance or credits before treating this lane as a secret-delivery or config failure.
Re-probe `venice-uncensored` after the balance is restored and keep the lane demoted until provider-specific completion succeeds again.
If a post-credit probe still fails, route the lane back into the VAULT repair packet instead of assuming balance was the only blocker.
notes: Venice remains configured as an API lane.

Latest live VAULT provider probe ended in `request_failed` on 2026-04-16 because Venice reported insufficient balance, so keep the lane demoted until credits are restored and completion succeeds again.

providers.label: Z.ai API
verification_steps: Keep the Z.ai provider-specific probe in the regular VAULT LiteLLM evidence refresh now that the `glm-4.7` lane completes successfully again.
Treat any future Z.ai breakage as either secret-delivery regression or upstream auth failure, not as missing-env debt unless the VAULT env audit drops `ZAI_API_KEY` again.
If Athanor later changes the canonical Z.ai served alias, update the provider contract explicitly instead of letting the lane drift back into ambiguous remediation state.
notes: The 2026-04-16 live VAULT provider probe now succeeds through the `glm-4.7` alias after restoring `ZAI_API_KEY` to the running LiteLLM container.

providers.label: OpenRouter API
verification_steps: Treat this as an auth-mode or credential-validity mismatch even though OPENROUTER_API_KEY is now present in the container env.
Recreate or redeploy the `litellm` container only if the VAULT env surface changes again.
Re-probe the served model `openrouter` and keep the lane demoted until the provider-specific completion succeeds.
notes: Official OpenRouter pricing is pay-as-you-go and states there is no markup over listed provider model prices.

Latest live VAULT provider probe still classified this lane as auth_failed after OPENROUTER_API_KEY was restored, so the remaining issue is upstream auth mode or credential validity rather than pure env absence.
Goose is the main shell-level operator surface in the target stack.
Codex Desktop remains a client surface, not the center of gravity.

recommendation_summaries.subject: protocol-first-builder-kernel
recommendation_summaries.summary: Advance the highest-priority active proof lane next.

recommendation_summaries.subject: foundry-bulk-harvest
recommendation_summaries.summary: Harvest Foundry bulk capacity while preserving one interactive GPU-equivalent reserve.
recommendation_summaries.reason: Foundry has the largest harvestable local envelope and should absorb eval, audit, and background transform work after operator reserve is protected.

recommendation_summaries.subject: sovereign_coder
recommendation_summaries.summary: Route private implementation and migrations to the sovereign local coding lane first.
recommendation_summaries.reason: It preserves cloud budget, aligns with sunk-cost local compute, and keeps private code on the local path.

recommendation_summaries.subject: formal_eval_run
recommendation_summaries.summary: Clean up the highest-frequency readiness gap before widening automation.
recommendation_summaries.reason: The most common missing readiness evidence right now is `formal_eval_run`.
